evaluation_summary.actors: The 'actors' list in the provided JSON is empty. The documentation describes numerous actors (e.g., Users, Super Apps, Sentinels, DAO, Pool Admins, Pool Members, Developers) that are not present, indicating a complete lack of extraction for this category. Therefore, it fails on completeness. Traceability, schema compliance, and consistency cannot be evaluated for an empty list.
evaluation_summary.assets: The 'assets' list in the provided JSON is empty. The documentation details various assets (e.g., Super Tokens, ERC20 Tokens, NFTs, Smart Contract code, User Data, Buffer Deposits, Governance Tokens) that have not been extracted. This indicates a complete failure in completeness for this category. Traceability, schema compliance, and consistency are not evaluable for an empty list.
evaluation_summary.components: The 'components' list in the provided JSON is empty. The documentation outlines many components (e.g., Superfluid Host, Super Token Contracts, Agreement Contracts like CFAv1/GDAv1, Forwarder Contracts, Sentinel Nodes, Superfluid Dashboard, Subgraph) that are missing. This shows a complete lack of extraction for components, failing the completeness criterion. Traceability, schema compliance, and consistency cannot be assessed for an empty list.
evaluation_summary.data_flows: The 'data_flows' list in the provided JSON is empty. The documentation describes numerous interactions and data movements (e.g., stream creation, token wrapping, callback execution, governance voting, liquidation processes) that are not captured. This signifies a complete omission in extracting data flows, failing the completeness test. Traceability, schema compliance, and consistency are not assessable for an empty list.
evaluation_summary.trust_boundaries: The 'trust_boundaries' list in the provided JSON is empty. The documentation implies several trust boundaries (e.g., between user wallets and smart contracts, between on-chain and off-chain components, between the protocol and its governance mechanism) that have not been identified. This indicates a complete failure to extract trust boundaries, thus failing on completeness. Traceability, schema compliance, and consistency cannot be evaluated for an empty list.
evaluation_summary.behaviors: The 'behaviors' list in the provided JSON is empty. The documentation details a wide range of system behaviors and use cases (e.g., money streaming, token distribution, vesting, staking, batch transactions) that are entirely missing from the extraction. This represents a complete failure in terms of completeness for this category. Traceability, schema compliance, and consistency are not evaluable for an empty list.

findings.description: Actor 'User' (initiating transactions like wrapping, streaming, deploying contracts) is described throughout the documentation (e.g., doc_2, doc_13, doc_44) but not extracted.
Actor 'Super App' (smart contracts reacting to Superfluid events) is a core concept (doc_3, doc_21, doc_22, doc_24) but not extracted.
Actor 'Sentinel' (nodes monitoring and liquidating streams) is described (doc_1, doc_6, doc_18, doc_19) but not extracted.
Actor 'DAO (Decentralized Autonomous Organization)' (governing the Superfluid protocol) is mentioned (doc_11, doc_41) but not extracted.
Actor 'Pool Admin' (managing distribution pools and units) is described (doc_5, doc_28, doc_30) but not extracted.
Actor 'Pool Member' (recipients in a distribution pool) is described (doc_5, doc_28, doc_30) but not extracted.
Actor 'Developer' (building on Superfluid, deploying tokens/apps) is an implicit actor targeted by much of the documentation (e.g., doc_13, doc_22, doc_26) but not extracted.
Actor 'PIC (Patrician in Charge)' (participant in TOGA for liquidations) is described (doc_1, doc_18) but not extracted.
Asset 'Super Token' (e.g., USDCx, DAIx, ETHx - the core programmable tokens) is fundamental to the protocol (doc_1, doc_2, doc_7, doc_39) but not extracted.
Asset 'ERC20 Token' (underlying tokens that can be wrapped, e.g., USDC, DAI) is mentioned (doc_7, doc_14, doc_55) but not extracted.
Asset 'Native Asset' (e.g., ETH, MATIC - can be wrapped into Native Super Tokens) is mentioned (doc_39, doc_55) but not extracted.
Asset 'NFT (Non-Fungible Token)' (e.g., Tradeable Cashflow NFT, ConstantOutflowNFT, PoolAdminNFT) is mentioned (doc_3, doc_24, doc_32, doc_61) but not extracted.
Asset 'User Data' (bytes attachable to Superfluid operations) is described (doc_1, doc_21, doc_37, doc_51) but not extracted.
Asset 'Buffer Deposit' (funds locked when creating a stream) is described (doc_1, doc_6, doc_18) but not extracted.
Asset 'TOGA Stake' (funds locked by PIC in the TOGA contract) is described (doc_1, doc_18) but not extracted.
Asset 'Private Keys' (used by users/sentinels to sign transactions) are implicitly critical assets (doc_13, doc_19) but not extracted.
Component 'Superfluid Host Contract' (core protocol contract) is central to the architecture (doc_1, doc_4, doc_23, doc_45, doc_61) but not extracted.
Component 'Super Token Contract' (instance for each Super Token) is described (doc_7, doc_39, doc_59) but not extracted.
Component 'Constant Flow Agreement (CFAv1) Contract' (manages money streams) is described (doc_1, doc_21, doc_61, doc_63) but not extracted.
Component 'General Distribution Agreement (GDAv1) Contract' (manages distribution pools) is described (doc_28, doc_29, doc_61, doc_63) but not extracted.
Component 'Super Token Factory Contract' (deploys Super Tokens) is described (doc_41, doc_42, doc_61) but not extracted.
Component 'CFAv1Forwarder Contract' (interface for client-side stream operations) is described (doc_10, doc_44, doc_50, doc_56) but not extracted.
Component 'GDAv1Forwarder Contract' (interface for client-side pool operations) is described (doc_10, doc_44, doc_46, doc_57) but not extracted.
Component 'Sentinel Node Software' (run by Sentinels for liquidations) is described (doc_1, doc_18, doc_19) but not extracted.
Component 'Superfluid Dashboard' (web UI for interacting with the protocol) is mentioned (doc_2, doc_16, doc_26, doc_53, doc_64) but not extracted.
Component 'Superfluid Subgraph' (for querying protocol data) is described (doc_15, doc_20, doc_48, doc_52, doc_54, doc_62) but not extracted.
Component 'Vesting Scheduler Contract' (for automated vesting) is described (doc_17, doc_64) but not extracted.
Component 'MacroForwarder Contract' (for batching transactions) is described (doc_45, doc_49) but not extracted.
Data flow 'User creates a money stream to Receiver via CFAv1Forwarder' is a primary use case (doc_44, doc_50) but not extracted.
Data flow 'Super App contract executes callback logic upon receiving a stream' is a key feature (doc_3, doc_21, doc_24) but not extracted.
Data flow 'Sentinel liquidates an insolvent stream by interacting with CFAv1 and TOGA contracts' is described (doc_18, doc_19) but not extracted.
Data flow 'User wraps ERC20 token into Super Token via Super Token contract' is described (doc_7, doc_55) but not extracted.
Data flow 'Pool Admin updates member units in a Distribution Pool via GDAv1Forwarder or directly' is described (doc_30, doc_47) but not extracted.
Data flow 'User queries Superfluid Subgraph for account stream data' is described (doc_48, doc_52, doc_54) but not extracted.
Data flow 'Vesting Scheduler creates a stream to a recipient after cliff period' is described (doc_17, doc_64) but not extracted.
Data flow 'User executes batch operations via MacroForwarder calling a UserDefinedMacro contract' is described (doc_45, doc_49) but not extracted.
Trust boundary 'User Wallet <-> Superfluid Smart Contracts (Host, Forwarders, Tokens)' is fundamental (doc_44, doc_50) but not extracted.
Trust boundary 'Superfluid Protocol (On-chain Contracts) <-> Off-chain Sentinel Nodes' is described (doc_18, doc_19) but not extracted.
Trust boundary 'Superfluid Protocol (On-chain Contracts) <-> Superfluid Governance (DAO)' is described (doc_11, doc_41) but not extracted.
Trust boundary 'Superfluid Protocol (On-chain Contracts) <-> Off-chain Subgraph Indexers/Query Nodes' is described (doc_48, doc_52) but not extracted.
Trust boundary 'Super App Contract <-> Superfluid Host Contract (Callbacks)' is described (doc_3, doc_21) but not extracted.
Trust boundary 'Client Application (e.g., Superfluid Dashboard) <-> User Wallet (e.g., MetaMask)' is implicit in SDK guides (doc_44, doc_50) but not extracted.
Behavior 'Money Streaming (Create/Update/Delete Flow)' is a core functionality (doc_2, doc_6, doc_34, doc_36, doc_50) but not extracted.
Behavior 'Token Distribution (Instant/Streaming via Pools)' is a core functionality (doc_5, doc_28, doc_29, doc_47) but not extracted.
Behavior 'Token Wrapping/Unwrapping (ERC20 to SuperToken and vice-versa)' is described (doc_7, doc_14, doc_55) but not extracted.
Behavior 'Super App Reactivity (Callbacks on stream events)' is described (doc_3, doc_21, doc_24) but not extracted.
Behavior 'Stream Liquidation (by Sentinels for insolvent accounts)' is described (doc_1, doc_18, doc_19) but not extracted.
Behavior 'Automated Vesting (Scheduled token release with cliffs)' is described (doc_17, doc_64) but not extracted.
Behavior 'Batching Transactions (using Macros and MacroForwarder)' is described (doc_45, doc_49) but not extracted.
Behavior 'Access Control Management (granting/revoking flow operator permissions)' is described (doc_37, doc_51) but not extracted.
Behavior 'Deploying Super Tokens (Pure, Wrapper, Custom)' is described (doc_41, doc_42, doc_43) but not extracted.
Behavior 'Querying Protocol Data (using Subgraph or Explorer)' is described (doc_48, doc_52, doc_54) but not extracted.
